key_insight: SciReasoner is a multimodal foundation model that encodes molecular, protein, and crystal structure as inspectable tokens to improve prediction and reasoning across scientific tasks.
clinical_relevance: By improving structure-based biological reasoning, it may support better target annotation and downstream therapeutic discovery workflows, but it is not itself a drug candidate or clinical tool.
method: Discretizes coordinates, topologies, and periodic connectivities into a unified structure-aware vocabulary.
Uses structural tokens as addressable evidence during reasoning across proteins, small molecules, and inorganic crystals.
Evaluates performance on homology-controlled GO prediction, retrosynthesis, and materials benchmarks.
evidence_signal: Across 86 benchmarks, the model was state-of-the-art on 67 tasks, and expert judges preferred or found comparable its reasoning traces in 98% of cases.
watch_item: Performance is benchmark-driven, so real-world utility will depend on prospective validation, robustness to noisy structures, and careful integration into scientific workflows.
paper_title: Accurate, Interdisciplinary and Transparent Structure-property Understanding with Deep Native Structural Reasoning
